title: The term ‘dual-use’ is misleading and dangerous
summary: The article argues that the term 'dual-use' in space technology is misleading, as technology's purpose depends on intent and context rather than inherent characteristics. It emphasizes that the ethical and political implications of technology use are what matter, not the technology itself.

title: BAE microchip designed for strategic defense missions passes radiation test
summary: BAE Systems' Endura microchip has successfully passed radiation tests, making it suitable for classified and missile-defense satellite programs. The company is positioning the chip for strategic defense missions.

title: Connected Aircraft Fleet Set to Exceed 70,000 by 2035
summary: The In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) market is projected to see long-term growth, with the number of connected aircraft exceeding 70,000 by 2035. IFC penetration is also expected to increase from its current 48%.

title: Centre for Space Futures, Novaspace and SpaceTech Gulf  Sign Agreement to Develop Global Space Capability Mapping Dashboard
summary: The Centre for Space Futures (CSF), Novaspace, and SpaceTech Gulf signed an agreement to develop a global space capability mapping dashboard. The collaboration aims to enhance international space capabilities.

title: Tianwen-2 arrives at asteroid Kamo’oalewa, first image revealed
summary: China's Tianwen-2 spacecraft has reached the near-Earth asteroid Kamoʻoalewa, capturing its first image which reveals a small, elongated rocky body. The mission, launched on May 29, 2025, aims to study and potentially return samples from the asteroid.

title: NASA and Small Business Administration partner on funding key space technologies
summary: NASA and the U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) are collaborating to secure funding for companies developing essential space technologies. The partnership aims to attract investment in critical space components.

title: Airbus to build Aeolus-2 wind-monitoring satellite
summary: The European Space Agency has chosen Airbus Defence and Space to develop the Aeolus-2 satellite, which will succeed the current wind-monitoring mission. This initiative aims to enhance global wind observation capabilities.

title: GAO flags satellite costs, launch risks in Space Force portfolio
summary: The Government Accountability Office (GAO) has raised concerns about rising costs and launch risks for missile-warning satellites in the U.S. Space Force portfolio. Workforce reductions and gaps in digital engineering could further delay national security launches.

title: Pegasus launches Swift reboost mission
summary: A Pegasus XL rocket launched a mission to reboost NASA's Swift astrophysics spacecraft, potentially marking the final flight of the Pegasus rocket. The launch was reported by SpaceNews.

title: Chinese satellite manufacturer Hongqing raises $191 million
summary: Hongqing Technology, a satellite manufacturing affiliate of Landspace, raised $191 million in one of the largest funding rounds for a Chinese commercial satellite maker. The funding round was announced recently, highlighting growth in China's commercial space sector.

title: Japan's Hayabusa2 probe captures remarkable photo of a two-headed asteroid 62 million miles away
summary: Japan's Hayabusa2 probe captured a remarkable image of the double-lobed asteroid Torifune, located 62 million miles away, during a close approach on July 5.

title: Artemis moon astronauts visit Capitol Hill | Space photo of the day for July 6, 2026
summary: NASA's Artemis II astronauts visited Capitol Hill after completing a 10-day mission around the moon in April 2026. The visit highlights their historic journey and aims to engage with policymakers.

title: NASA will have to find a way to service its new alien-hunting space telescope
summary: NASA is exploring robotic servicing options for its upcoming Habitable Worlds Observatory, a space telescope designed to search for extraterrestrial life. This could involve autonomous robots performing maintenance and upgrades in orbit.

title: Astronomers discover radio signals coming from rare 'Blue Eye Pulsar' after decades of silence
summary: Astronomers have detected radio signals from the 'Blue Eye Pulsar,' a rare neutron star that had been silent for decades. This discovery marks a significant breakthrough in pulsar research.

title: 'Once-in-a-millennium' asteroid flyby will be visible to much of the world in 2029
summary: A rare asteroid flyby of Apophis in 2029 will be visible to 90% of the world's population. This once-in-a-millennium event offers a unique opportunity for global observation.

title: NASA tests advanced new Mars rover prototype in the California desert (video)
summary: NASA is testing a new Mars rover prototype in the California desert to improve autonomous navigation and terrain handling. The advanced robot aims to overcome challenges that previously stalled older rovers on lunar or Martian surfaces.

title: 10 best Spanish cities to see the total solar eclipse 2026
summary: Spain is highlighted as a prime location to view the total solar eclipse on August 12, 2026, with several major cities like A Coruña, Bilbao, Madrid, and Zaragoza offering optimal viewing spots.

title: America 250: From 1776 to the moon and beyond (A Space.com series)
summary: Space.com reflects on U.S. space achievements since 1776 and explores future possibilities as America celebrates its 250th anniversary. The series highlights the nation's journey from early exploration to potential lunar and beyond.

title: NASA’s CAPSTONE Completes Extended Mission Testing Lunar Technologies
summary: NASA's CAPSTONE mission has completed its extended mission to test lunar technologies, validating autonomous navigation and communication systems for future Moon missions. The spacecraft was designed to operate without direct Earth connections, supporting NASA's goal of a sustained human presence on the Moon.

title: NASA Webb Uncovers Unusual Galaxy Shaped by Cosmic Collision
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope reveals a transformed view of the galaxy Centaurus A, uncovering complex structures previously obscured by dust. The new infrared images highlight the galaxy's intricate details shaped by cosmic collisions.

title: Examining Algal Blooms in Blue Mesa
summary: Researchers used satellite data to link harmful algal blooms at Blue Mesa Reservoir to warm water and low water levels. The study highlights environmental impacts at one of Colorado's largest reservoirs.

title: NASA’s Hubble Spies Stellar Sparkler for July 4th
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured a festive image of stars resembling a red, white, and blue sparkler. The image was released to celebrate the U.S. Independence Day on July 4th.

title: NASA’s Hubble Captures Crimson Cloud Sparkling with White, Blue Stars
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a crimson gas cloud sparkling with blue and white stars. The image highlights the vibrant colors and stellar activity within the nebula.

title: NASA’s Hubble Spots Star-Spangled Cosmic Scene
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the globular cluster Messier 3 (M3), showcasing over 500,000 stars in red, white, and blue hues. This cosmic scene highlights the vibrant and dense stellar population within the cluster.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Breaks Agency Streaming Record
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission achieved record-breaking viewership with over 149.4 million views across its live streams, showcasing strong global engagement. The mission's live coverage, including the launch and splashdown, set unprecedented records for the agency.

title: Good Morning, Earth!
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams captured a photo of an orbital sunrise from the International Space Station on June 26, 2026. The ISS completes 16 orbits around Earth daily, experiencing 16 sunrises and sunsets.

title: NASA’s Webb Reveals Stars Sparking to Life in Cosmic Celebration
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope captured infrared images of stars forming in the FS Tau star system, revealing previously unseen features behind thick dust. The image also showcased numerous background galaxies, adding to the cosmic spectacle.

title: What’s Up: July 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: NASA shares skywatching tips for July 2026, highlighting a Moon-and-planets meetup, a returning comet, Milky Way visibility, and Saturn's rings at a new angle.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station aims to be the world's first commercial space station.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are making progress toward this goal.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative in June 2026. The contracts aim to support the development of landers for future lunar missions.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent New Glenn anomaly that caused significant damage. The company aims to restore the facility to operational status swiftly.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory and the final launch of an Atlas satellite are scheduled this week. Multiple orbital launches are planned globally.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to launch the Swift Boost Mission to save a NASA space telescope. This marks the rocket's first mission since 2021.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, aiding future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The data will help scientists study the galaxy's structure and dynamics.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Meanwhile, Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new modifications.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate the public about the shuttle's legacy after 25 flights.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's space launches include six orbital missions worldwide, featuring the first Pegasus XL launch alongside Falcon 9 and Chinese missions. The Pegasus XL launch is highlighted as a notable event in the launch manifest.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting upcoming events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025. These events aim to inform industry about planned activities, priorities, and opportunities across ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its potential versatility and impact on future missions. The innovation could streamline operations in space exploration and satellite servicing.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. This initiative supports European space science activities by involving research institutes.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency (ESA) test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section supports ESA's missions with innovative solutions.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news discusses the durability and sensitivity of a space-related component or system. The image accompanying the news likely depicts this sturdy and sensitive technology.

title: Exclusive: Ravee Optics Raises $6M Seed to Build Compact Laser Terminals
summary: Ravee Optics has raised $6 million in a seed funding round to develop compact laser communication terminals. Their technology aims to make laser communications more accessible with lighter, smaller, and scalable solutions.

title: The Biggest Fundraises of H1 2026
summary: The article highlights the most significant fundraises in the space industry for the first half of 2026, emphasizing how increased investment is boosting rocket launches and payload capacity worldwide.

title: Inside the Deepening Ties Between Europe and the Middle East
summary: Europe's space industry is strengthening ties with Middle Eastern companies amid deteriorating geopolitical relations with the US, creating new opportunities for collaboration. This shift highlights Europe's push for greater sovereignty in space exploration and technology.

title: True Anomaly Approaches, Images Rocket Lab Craft
summary: True Anomaly successfully captured and delivered images of a Rocket Lab spacecraft within 61 hours of receiving the order, beating the 72-hour deadline. The mission highlights True Anomaly's rapid-response capabilities in space imaging.

title: Exclusive: Austria Taps R-Space for Its Second Military Sat
summary: Austria has selected R-Space for its second military satellite mission, named Aurora. This mission is part of a series of government-backed demonstrations to test new orbital technologies.

title: Exclusive: Lunar Forge Unveils Plans to Build Lunar Infrastructure Out of Dust
summary: Lunar Forge has revealed plans to construct lunar infrastructure using regolith to reduce costs for fission reactors. The company aims to use lunar dust to build support structures for reactor shielding.

title: NASA Praises Blue Origin’s Mishap Progress, Announces Moon Base Contracts
summary: NASA praised Blue Origin's progress in addressing mishaps and announced contracts for moon base missions. The agency plans to use updated versions of existing lander designs for monthly landings with NASA payloads.

title: Payload Field Guide: CLPS Missions
summary: The article discusses CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) missions, focusing on lunar landers and their role in upcoming Moon missions. It serves as a guide to understanding these payload missions.

title: Exclusive: Satellogic Expands AI Apps with SpaceKnow Partnership
summary: Satellogic partnered with SpaceKnow to develop AI applications using Satellogic's satellite imagery. This collaboration aims to enhance geospatial intelligence analysis capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Satcom Market With $8B Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab has entered the satellite communications market through an $8 billion deal with Iridium. This marks a significant expansion for the company into commercial space applications.

title: Amazon Leo Constellation Ready to Support Initial Service After ULA Mission
summary: Amazon's LEO constellation has achieved enough satellites in orbit to support initial service across key latitudes following a ULA launch. This marks a significant milestone for the company's satellite internet ambitions.

title: Isar Aerospace Lands Big Win With Planet Launch Contract
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a launch contract with Planet to deploy one of its next-generation Pelican satellites as early as late 2026. This marks a significant milestone for the German launcher developer.

title: Mitsubishi Electric to Acquire Infostellar
summary: Mitsubishi Electric has announced its intention to acquire Infostellar, a prominent company in the Japanese space sector. The acquisition aims to strengthen Mitsubishi Electric's position in the industry.

title: Vodafone Ireland Conducts Emergency Services D2D Call With AST SpaceMobile JV
summary: Vodafone Ireland successfully conducted a test call with AST SpaceMobile's satellite constellation to evaluate connectivity for emergency and first responders. The test is part of efforts to enhance emergency services communication.

title: The Internet in Space: The Apps that Dominate Satellite Traffic and Why it Matters
summary: The article discusses the applications dominating satellite traffic in space and their significance for non-government use. It explores the growing importance of satellite networks as thousands of new satellites are launched.

title: Dish Satellite TV and Wireless Businesses File for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
summary: Dish DBS and Dish Wireless filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy to facilitate the transition of their wireless business after spectrum transactions. The move aims to restructure operations while continuing services.

title: Ovzon Wins New Defense Deal with European NATO Nation
summary: Ovzon secured a $7.1 million defense contract with a European NATO country for its satellite communications solution. The deal is valued at 74 million Swedish krona.

title: Unseenlabs Orders 2 Cubesats from GomSpace
summary: Unseenlabs has ordered two additional cubesats from GomSpace for 2.2 million euros ($2.5 million). The contract was announced by GomSpace on Wednesday.

title: Lite Coms Hires Calspan Exec Greg Campbell as President
summary: Lite Coms has appointed Greg Campbell, a former Calspan executive, as its new president to oversee daily operations and expand manufacturing capabilities.

title: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines Win NASA Lunar Delivery Missions
summary: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines have been awarded NASA contracts to deliver payloads to the Moon as part of efforts to establish a lunar base. This marks a significant step in NASA's lunar exploration and commercial partnerships.
